title: Realized volatility breakout filter
hypothesis: A transition from compressed realized volatility to expanding realized volatility can improve strategy selection: carry and basis candidates should be down-ranked during stress transitions, while momentum alerts may receive higher priority when expansion is confirmed by volume.

inputs: spot and perp candles or trade-derived bars
mark price series for perp stress checks
trade volume or trade count for confirmation
base strategy candidates to be filtered
fee and turnover assumptions for opportunity-cost accounting
entry_rules: realized volatility was below low percentile over the compression lookback
realized volatility crosses high percentile or absolute threshold over breakout window
volume or trade count confirms participation is not stale data
carry and basis candidates are down-ranked during fresh breakout state
momentum candidates are only up-ranked when direction and volume confirm
exit_rules: realized volatility returns to neutral band
base strategy risk controls override filter output
manual review rejects current market state

test_plan: Build 1m, 5m and 1h realized volatility features from BTCUSDT, ETHUSDT and SOLUSDT spot/perp data.
Test low-vol compression followed by high-vol breakout across 1d, 3d and 7d rolling percentiles.
Apply the filter to funding_carry_vol_filter, quarterly_basis_convergence and oi_confirmed_momentum fixture outputs.
Track avoided drawdown, missed opportunity and net edge after costs separately.
Run walk-forward threshold checks across symbols and months.
Keep the strategy as a filter until it improves at least one existing evaluator after opportunity cost.
risk_controls: Default output is filter decision, not standalone trade.
Do not use volatility expansion as permission for high leverage.
Require volume confirmation and fresh data for breakout state.
Measure missed profitable trades as an explicit cost of the filter.
No production config changes, auto-deployment or live trading.
